name: sr_cohort_surfacing_ask_list
notes: VT-490 cohort-surfacing (Done): build_sales_recovery_context now supplies the per-customer dormant-cohort rows (customer_id/display_name/days_since_last_sale — CL-390/CL-425-gated, minimum-necessary fields only, no phone/email) to the brain, so a direct 'who exactly are they' ask should get a grounded, substantive answer drawn from the real seeded cohort — never an 'insufficient_data' non-answer (the pre-VT-490 gap), never a fabricated list beyond what was actually seeded, never silence. Run: `setup --onboarded --seed-lapsed-customers 10 --name convo-harness-<id>` then `script <tenant> sr_cohort_surfacing_ask_list.json`.

steps.message: who exactly are the customers that have gone quiet on me? give me a few names to start with
steps.note: Direct cohort-surfacing ask (not a plan ask). Expect a grounded, substantive reply drawn from the real seeded dormant cohort — never a generic insufficient-data brush-off, never invented names/customers beyond the seed, never silence. Manual review (json-report/judge transcript) verdicts grounding quality — no phone/email should ever appear (CL-390).

steps.message: and how many lapsed customers do I have in total?
steps.note: VT-632 (Fazal 2026-07-09: lapsed = no purchase in 45d): the LAPSED count is the dormant subset, NOT the total seeded. The seeder makes n_lapsed = n - n//4 = 10 - 2 = 8 customers ~120-295d dormant (the other 2 are recent, ~2-10d). Corrected from the old ['10'] which encoded the TOTAL (customer_count) — the opus judge flagged it as answering total-vs-lapsed. Now fast-paths to lapsed_count (deterministic, 45d).
